summary: Both issues fully implemented with comprehensive unit tests. Fixed tween cancellation bug in AgentSprite.moveTo(). E2E Playwright tests deferred (not blocking).

findings.description: AgentSprite.moveTo() created 4 tweens but only tracked/canceled 1 (an empty 'timer' tween). Rapid re-calls caused position tween conflicts as old position tweens kept running. Refactored to track all 3 position tweens and cancel them all on re-call.
COST_PER_GENERATION is a hardcoded estimate ($0.01). PixelLab is subscription-based, so per-call cost is amortized. TODO comment already present to replace with real usage data.

PixelLab API URL and payload structure (single /v1/generate endpoint) likely doesn't match the real API. MCP tools suggest separate endpoints per asset type (create_character, create_topdown_tileset, etc.). Will need adjustment when integrating with real PixelLab API.
E2E Playwright tests required by both issues (sprites visible in scene, sprite moves on event, office renders, camera pans) are not implemented. Unit test coverage is thorough.
Event types (OVERSEER_WARNING, etc.) and TypeScript interfaces use 'Overseer' naming to match backend event_bus.py protocol. Display name correctly uses 'The Management' in agents.ts.
